title: Introduce billing event retry orchestration
summary: Coordinate billing retries across the API route and billing service so failed webhook processing can recover safely.
requestedOutcome: Persist retry context, expose retry behavior through the billing route, and preserve operator visibility for failures.
constraints: Keep the existing billing webhook contract stable for clients.
Do not lose failed retry attempts during rollout.
openQuestions: Should retry state live in the billing service or a shared persistence boundary?

signals: touches architecture-sensitive boundaries or quality concerns

requirements.title: Preserve route contract
requirements.description: Retry handling must not change the public billing webhook request shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing billing clients do not need to change.

requirements.title: Persist retry state
requirements.description: The system must persist enough retry context to resume failed billing events safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Operators can identify and replay failed retries.

requirements.title: Cover retry flow with tests
requirements.description: The implementation must add route and service tests that prove retry behavior across the module boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Retry route and service coverage exist.
testImplications: Route and billing service integration coverage is required.

implementationNotes.statement: Retry state crosses the boundary between the billing worker and the API route, so operator visibility matters.

acceptanceCriteria: Billing retries can be resumed safely.
Route behavior remains stable for callers.
suggestedSequence: Wire retry orchestration into the billing route.
